# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern Single Page Application (SPA) built with React 18 and TypeScript. It follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns:

- **Frontend**: React SPA with client-side routing
- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Real-time)
- **State Management**: TanStack Query for server state, React Context for global state
- **UI Framework**: Radix UI primitives with Tailwind CSS styling
- **Build System**: Vite with SWC for fast development and optimized production builds

## Key Architectural Patterns

### Authentication & Authorization
- Supabase Auth for user authentication
- Role-based access control with route protection
- JWT tokens for API authentication
- Password reset and invite flows

### Data Fetching
- TanStack Query for caching, background updates, and optimistic updates
- Supabase client for database operations
- Real-time subscriptions for live data updates

### Component Organization
- Feature-based component organization (employee-profile/, projects/, etc.)
- Shared UI components in ui/ directory
- Layout components for consistent navigation and structure
- Custom hooks for business logic encapsulation

### Routing & Navigation
- React Router with protected routes
- Role-based route access control
- Nested routing for detail pages
- Programmatic navigation with redirects

## Database Schema (Supabase)

The application uses PostgreSQL with the following main entities:
- employees (user profiles and organizational data)
- attendance_records (time tracking)
- leave_requests (vacation/sick leave)
- payroll_records (salary and compensation)
- projects (project management)
- evaluations (performance reviews)
- invoices (billing)
- hr_policies (company policies)
- settings tables (departments, roles, etc.)

## Key Files and Their Purposes

### Core Application Files
- `src/main.tsx`: Application entry point, renders App component
- `src/App.tsx`: Main application component with routing configuration
- `src/index.css`: Global styles and Tailwind imports

### Authentication
- `src/hooks/useAuth.tsx`: Authentication state management
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Role-based permission checking

### Data Layer
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`: Database type definitions

### UI Components
- `src/components/ui/`: Base UI components (buttons, forms, tables, etc.)
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main application layout with sidebar
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar

### Business Logic
- `src/lib/`: Utility functions for business logic
  - `leave-utils.ts`: Leave balance calculations
  - `review-schedule.ts`: Performance review scheduling
  - `notifications.ts`: Notification system
  - `format-date.ts`: Date formatting utilities

### Pages/Routes
- `src/pages/Dashboard.tsx`: Main dashboard
- `src/pages/Employees.tsx`: Employee listing
- `src/pages/EmployeeProfile.tsx`: Individual employee details
- `src/pages/Settings.tsx`: System configuration
- `src/pages/Payroll.tsx`: Payroll management
- `src/pages/LeaveManagement.tsx`: Leave request handling

## Development Workflow

### Build Commands
- `npm run dev`: Start development server
- `npm run build`: Production build
- `npm run test`: Run unit tests
- `npm run lint`: Code linting

### Environment Configuration
- Environment variables prefixed with `VITE_` for client-side access
- Supabase configuration in `.env` file
- Separate configs for development/production

## Security Considerations

- All API calls authenticated via Supabase
- Row Level Security (RLS) policies on database tables
- Client-side validation with Zod schemas
- Protected routes with role checking
- Secure password handling for invites/resets

## Performance Optimizations

- Vite for fast development builds
- Code splitting with dynamic imports
- TanStack Query for intelligent caching
- Optimized bundle with tree shaking
- Lazy loading of routes and components

## Testing Strategy

- Unit tests with Vitest
- E2E tests with Playwright
- Component testing with React Testing Library
- API integration testing

## Deployment

- Built for static hosting (Vercel, Netlify, etc.)
- Environment-specific builds
- CDN-ready assets
- Database migrations via Supabase

## Common Patterns

### Data Fetching
```typescript
const { data, isLoading } = useQuery({
  queryKey: ['entity', id],
  queryFn: () => supabase.from('table').select('*').eq('id', id),
});
```

### Form Handling
```typescript
const form = useForm<Schema>({
  resolver: zodResolver(schema),
  defaultValues: {},
});
```

### Component Structure
```typescript
interface Props {
  // props
}

const Component = ({ prop }: Props) => {
  // component logic
  return <div>Content</div>;
};

export default Component;
```
